title: 2024 Coolest Tool Award
description: The fifth edition of the Coolest Tool Award happened at Wikimania in Katowice, Poland in August 2024. See https://meta.wikimedia.org/wiki/Coolest_Tool_Award/2024 for more details.

tools.name: toolforge-view-it
tools.title: View it!
tools.description:
View it! is a tool to show Wikipedia editors and readers relevant Wikimedia Commons media depicting—or otherwise related to—the article they are reading. 

https://meta.wikimedia.org/wiki/View_it!_Tool

tools.name: isa
tools.title: ISA Tool : Easily Add Information to Images on Wikimedia Commons
tools.description:
ISA is a multilingual, mobile-first micro-contributions tool, that makes it easy for people to add structured data to images on Wikimedia Commons.
Logged-in Wikimedians can create dedicated campaigns with the goal of improving one or several specific categories of images on Wikimedia Commons, within a certain time frame. Any logged-in wikimedian can then participate to the campaign adding structured metadata. 
The tool is designed to be a host of small competitions. In particular, the tool provides stats that allow to identify the best contributors to the said campaign real time ! Participation info is provided in a nice visual format that can be directly used to include in coordinator report or used to communicate the outcome of the competition on social media, turning the campaign into a communication tool. Data about each campaign participation is also available for download into cvs format for any further statistical uses.

tools.name: enwiki-קיפודנחש-cat-a-lot
tools.title: Cat-a-lot
tools.description: {{anchor|Cat-a-lot}}Extremely powerful category editing tool from Commons. Works with regular articles, as well as files. This is a wrapper that sets the setting variable to enable non-file categorization, and loads the latest Cat-a-lot from Commons.

tools.name: integraality
tools.title: inteGraality
tools.description: Generate dashboards of property coverage for a given part of Wikidata.

tools.name: toolforge-campwiz
tools.title: a tool designed to streamline and manage Wikipedia edit-a-thons
tools.description: CampWiz is a tool designed to streamline and manage Wikipedia edit-a-thons, particularly those focused on cultural diversity and gender inclusivity, like the Feminism and Folklore Editathon, by offering features for event management, submission tracking, and evaluation
